verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-resident HSP70 chaperone that retains via the KDEL retrieval sequence. However, substantial evidence documents its translocation to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane — a phenomenon well-characterized on tumor cells (cancer ecto-GRP78), on virally infected cells, and during ER stress / unfolded protein response. On cancer cells, surface GRP78 acts as a co-receptor and is targeted by clinical-stage antibodies, ADCs, and CAR-T constructs (e.g., MT-3724, PAT-DX1, various anti-GRP78 antibodies). It also appears on the surface of activated macrophages and during apoptosis/ICD. The mechanism of PM translocation is not fully defined (possibly via secretory pathway leakage or active translocation overcoming KDEL retention), but surface display is wash-resistant and detected robustly by flow cytometry and surface biotinylation on stressed/tumor cells. This is a canonical example of a cell-state-induced ecto-chaperone qualifying as contextual/cell_state_induced.
